title: Hybrid digital-analog MMWAVE repeater/relay with full duplex
abstract_clean: A system for transmitting millimeter wave signals includes at least one repeater for communicating with a plurality of remote locations over millimeter wave communications links. The at least one repeater further includes multiple input multiple output (MIMO) transmission circuitry for transmitting signals and receiving signals over the millimeter wave communications links to the plurality of remote locations. The at least one repeater further includes full-duplex transmission circuitry for processing the transmitted signals and the received signals over the millimeter wave communications links using full-duplex communications with the plurality of remote locations. The at least one repeater relays the millimeter wave signals between at least a first millimeter wave transceiver at a first one of the plurality of remote locations and a second millimeter wave transceiver at a second one of the plurality of remote locations.
claims_clean:
1. A system for transmitting millimeter wave signals, comprising:
at least one repeater for communicating with a plurality of remote locations over millimeter wave communications links, wherein the at least one repeater further comprises:
multiple input multiple output (MIMO) transmission circuitry for transmitting signals and receiving signals over the millimeter wave communications links to the plurality of remote locations;
full-duplex transmission circuitry for processing the transmitted signals and the received signals over the millimeter wave communications links using full-duplex communications with the plurality of remote locations; and

wherein the at least one repeater relays the millimeter wave signals between at least a first millimeter wave transceiver at a first one of the plurality of remote locations and a second millimeter wave transceiver at a second one of the plurality of remote locations.
2. The system of claim 1, wherein the MIMO transmission circuitry limits interference between the transmitted signals and the received signals.

3. The system of claim 1 further comprising orbital angular momentum (OAM) processing circuitry for applying a selected orbital angular momentum to the transmitted signals, wherein the selected orbital angular momentum limits interference between the transmitted signals and the received signals.
4. The system of claim 1, wherein the first millimeter wave transceiver is associated with an enodeB and the second millimeter wave transceiver is associated with customer premises equipment.

5. The system of claim 4, wherein the at least one repeater relays the millimeter wave signals between the first millimeter wave transceiver at the first one of the plurality of remote locations and the second millimeter wave transceiver at the second one of the plurality of remote locations and a third millimeter wave transceiver at a third one of the plurality of remote locations, the third millimeter wave transciever associated with a second customer premises equipment.

6. The system of claim 4 further comprising a passive reflector located between the at least one repeater and the customer premises equipment for reflecting the transmitted signals from the at least one repeater to the second millimeter wave transceiver associated with the customer premises equipment.

7. The system of claim 1, wherein the at least one repeater comprises a first repeater and a second repeater relaying the transmitted signals and the received signals therebetween, further wherein the first millimeter wave transceiver is associated with an enodeB and the second millimeter wave transceiver is associated with customer premises equipment.

8. A system for transmitting RF signals, comprising:
at least one repeater for communicating with a plurality of remote locations over RF communications links, wherein the at least one repeater further comprises:
multiple input multiple output (MIMO) transmission circuitry for transmitting signals and receiving signals over the RF communications links to the plurality of remote locations;
full-duplex transmission circuitry for processing the transmitted signals and the received signals over the RF communications links using full-duplex communications with the plurality of remote locations; and

wherein the at least one repeater relays the RF signals between at least a first RF transceiver at a first one of the plurality of remote locations and a second RF transceiver at a second one of the plurality of remote locations.
9. The system of claim 8, wherein the MIMO transmission circuitry limits interference between the transmitted signals and the received signals.

10. The system of claim 8 further comprising orbital angular momentum (OAM) processing circuitry for applying a selected orbital angular momentum to the transmitted signals, wherein the selected orbital angular momentum limits interference between the transmitted signals and the received signals.
11. The system of claim 8, wherein the first RF transceiver is associated with an enodeB and the second RF transceiver is associated with customer premises equipment.

12. The system of claim 11, wherein the at least one repeater relays the RF signals between the first RF transceiver at the first one of the plurality of remote locations and the second RF transceiver at the second one of the plurality of remote locations and a third RF transceiver at a third one of the plurality of remote locations, the third RF transceiver associated with a second customer premises equipment.

13. The system of claim 11 further comprising a passive reflector located between the at least one repeater and the customer premises equipment for reflecting the transmitted signals from the at least one repeater to the second RF transceiver associated with the customer premises equipment.

14. The system of claim 8, wherein the at least one repeater comprises a first repeater and a second repeater relaying the transmitted signals and the received signals therebetween, further wherein the first RF transceiver is associated with an enodeB and the second RF transceiver is associated with customer premises equipment.

16. The system of claim 15, wherein the at least one repeater further includes orbital angular momentum (OAM) processing circuitry for applying a selected orbital angular momentum to the transmitted signals, wherein the selected orbital angular momentum limits interference between the transmitted signals and the received signals.

17. The system of claim 15, wherein the at least one repeater further comprises a first repeater and a second repeater, the RF signals are bilaterally transmitted between the enodeB and the consumer premises equipment by relaying the RF signals through the first repeater and the second repeater.

18. The system of claim 15 further comprising a passive reflector located between the at least one repeater and customer premises equipment for reflecting the transmitted signals from the at least one repeater to the second RF transceiver associated with the customer premises equipment.

19. The system of claim 15, wherein the at least one repeater receives the RF signals from the enodeB and relays the RF signals to a plurality of RF transceivers located remotely form the at least one repeater.
20. The system of claim 15, wherein the RF signals comprise millimeter wave signals.
